full_text:
Tribus project update

25.05.2023

Introduction for Tribus

What’s new since the last group meeting

Napari - visualization for quality control

Browse images

Run Tribus
on one sample

Visualize results from previous runs

Napari workflow and challenges

Works fast on smaller masks (1-3 minutes for 17 celltypes) 
On laptops larger masks (~4GB) accumulate memory 
.Zarr still requires computation in numpy

Speaker notes:
Recall = (TP)/(TP+FN)
what proportion of actual Positives is correctly classified

5 Datasets are currently used to test Tribus

In house datasets: 
TMA
Relatively small dataset, good set for the single-cell annotation feature
tCyCIF
Tribus speed test
NKI_TMA_several
3 slides (TMA_18_810, TMA_34_504, TMA_44_810)

Public datasets: 
Stellar
With ground truth label, healthy tissue
CODEX multiplexed imaging, z-score normalised data
Colon: 110633 cells from 4 regions
https://doi.org/10.1038/s41592-022-01651-8
MIBI-TOF (possible dataset) 
Triple-negative breast cancer
With ground truth label and tiff images
Proves Tribus’s adaptability on various techniques
41 patients, 3,000–10,000 cells across patients
https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cell.2018.08.039

Pre-processed NKI_TMA_18_810 shows high marker quality

Log2 scale raw signals
Use 99 percentile to perform outlier truncation
Before: 460813 cells
After: 357236 cells (77.5%)
Perform Z-score and then Min-Max transformation in all channels

Tribus performs relatively good on identifying stromal cells, but need to be improved on immune cells

Rand score = 0.746
Accuracy score = 0.611
Precision score = 0.660
F1 score = 0.625

Marker expression heatmap reveals unclear classification on several markers

Modify the cell type description table in order to improve the accuracy.

Marker expression heatmap reveals unclear classification on several markers

Modify the cell type description table in order to improve the accuracy.

Tribus results on Stellar dataset in 2 levels

Global levels: Epithelial, Stromal, Lymphoid and Myeloid. 

Same pre processing method as NKI. (z-score and outlier removal)

We have to design our own cell type description table. 

But myeloid cells are a problem

Tribus results on Stellar dataset in 2 levels

Rand score = 0.868, Accuracy score = 0.424
Precision score = 0.781, F1 score = 0.520

Cell type description table design of Stellar shows Marker-marker correlation blocks - could become the metric of feature selection?

Found related research on scRNA-seq: https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-021-26085-2

Current ideas about improving accuracy

SOM

Scoring

Feature selection

Choose proper 
pre-processing methods

Threshold parameter tuning

Adjust logic table

Marker quality evaluation

How could you help Tribus?

Outlier filtering methods
Suggestions about pre-processing methods for CyTOF data
Discuss with Ziqi this feature selection paper: https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-021-26085-2 “DUBStepR is a scalable correlation-based feature selection method for accurately clustering single-cell data”
